name: LUNG TRANSPLANT
definition: ISCHEMIA-REPERFUSION INJURY = PULMONARY REIMPLANTATION RESPONSE noncardiogenic pulmonary edema = infiltrate appearing within 48 hours after BRONCHIOLITIS OBLITERANS SYNDROME (BOS)

demographics.Histo: fluid accumulation in interstitium consistent with noncardiogenic pulmonary edema
demographics.Location: perihilar areas + basal regions in transplanted lung

distribution: perihilar areas + basal regions in transplanted lung
discriminating_features: new increasing pleural effusion + septal thickening (most common, 90% specific, 68%
diffuse homogeneous infiltrate of entire allograft
perihilar haze / rapid uni- or bilateral heterogeneously dense interstitial and/or airspace

general: diffuse homogeneous infiltrate of entire allograft
perihilar haze / rapid uni- or bilateral heterogeneously dense interstitial and/or airspace
new increasing pleural effusion + septal thickening (most common, 90% specific, 68%
subpleural edema, peribronchial cuffing, airspace disease
CXR: diminished peripheral lung markings (= decreased peripheral vascularity)
thin irregular areas of increased opacity (= subsegmental atelectasis)
nodular / reticular opacities associated with peribronchial thickening
HRCT: mosaic lung attenuation (= regions of mixed hypo- and hyperattenuation)
presence of extraluminal air collections at anastomotic site (80%)
transient airway narrowing on expiratory CT
lobar / multilobar consolidation ← bacterial > fungal pathogens
diffuse heterogeneous air-space / ground-glass opacities ← parainfluenza + RSV +
bronchial wall thickening ← adenovirus, influenza virus, RSV
nodules ← fungal / unusual bacterial pathogens / CMV / septic emboli

clinical.Cx: may progress rapidly to respiratory failure + death
